prompt: Quick sanity check: does ARO have `if/else`?
model_raw:
<think>

</think>

No, ARO does not have `if/else` statements. Instead, ARO uses `When` to conditionally execute actions. For example:

```aro
When the <status> is<OK> {
    Return the<OK: status> for the <result>.
} else {
    Return the <error: status> for the <result>.
}
```
judge_reason: code block is missing the (Name: Activity) { ... } wrapper
expected: Not as a keyword. Use a `when` guard on a single statement (`Log "x" to <console> when <cond>.`) or a `match` block for multi-way branching.
